title: Generators & Yield
beginnerExplanation: A generator is a special function that returns an iterator. Instead of using 'return' to exit, it uses 'yield' to pause execution and send a value back, keeping its place for next time.
intermediateExplanation: When a generator yields, Python suspends its execution context, saving all local variables and registers on the heap. When next() is called, execution resumes from the line after the yield.
advancedExplanation: Generators can yield control to sub-generators using 'yield from sub()'. They support bidirectional communication via send(), throw(), and close() methods.

realWorldAnalogy.title: Video Game Checkpoint
realWorldAnalogy.description: Using 'return' is like restarting a game from the main menu. Using 'yield' is like hitting a checkpoint: the game saves exactly where you stand, so you can resume from that spot later.

mentalModel: A generator is a state machine disguised as a function.
visualDiagram: Call function -> executes to yield -> freezes frame & returns value -> next() -> resumes -> exits on return
executionWalkthrough: 1. Calling a generator function returns a generator object without running any code.
2. next() is called: execution enters the function and runs until it hits 'yield value'.
3. The value is returned, and the function is paused (stack frame preserved).
commonMistakes: Calling the generator function in a loop instead of the generator object (creates endless identical iterators!).
Expecting a generator function to run immediately on invocation.

debuggingMission.scenario: A player tries to loop over a generator but gets stuck in an infinite cycle of first elements.
debuggingMission.explanation: Calling get_nums() returns a new generator instance each time. Create it once, then call next() on it.

practiceQuestions.question: What keyword is used to pause a generator and return a value?
practiceQuestions.explanation: The yield keyword is responsible for returning a value and suspending the generator's execution state.

challengeQuestions.question: Which syntax delegates generator iteration to another sub-generator?
hints: yield followed by a word indicating source.

projectExercise.title: Sub-generator Delegation
projectExercise.description: Create a generator using yield from.
requirements: Define my_generator yielding from two sub-sequences.
projectExercise.starterCode:
def sub(): yield 1
def main(): yield from sub()

bossBattle.name: Sentinel of the Stream
bossBattle.description: Build a pipeline of generators to process energy waves.
bossBattle.mechanic: Use yield and yield from to pipe streams of numbers.

complexityAnalysis.timeComplexity: O(1) to yield an item, O(1) to resume.
complexityAnalysis.spaceComplexity: O(1) since values are generated on-the-fly without storage.
complexityAnalysis.description: Time complexity matches list traversal, but space drops from O(N) to O(1).

memoryImpact.allocationBytes: Minimal heap frame footprint (~120 bytes). Constant scale.
memoryImpact.optimizationNotes: Excellent for processing infinite data feeds like telemetry readings.

performanceNotes: Generator creation is extremely fast compared to pre-allocating large lists.

useCases: Stream processing in Big Data pipelines
